items.md: Previously unpublished interchange between Erik Svane and Carl Barks:
items.value: Previously unpublished interchange between Erik Svane and Carl Barks:

items.md: **ES:** Who do you think will inherit Scrooge's money?
items.value: ES: Who do you think will inherit Scrooge's money?

items.md: **ES:** Why would Huey, Dewey, and Louie receive it?
items.value: ES: Why would Huey, Dewey, and Louie receive it?

items.md: **CB:** Oh well, because they are so much more practical than Donald. In the later stories, as I developed those Duck people and the whole community of Duckburg and all of its problems, I began giving those kids much more intelligence than anybody else in Duckburg. And so I guess that when Uncle Scrooge passes on, he will leave all of his money to his three nephews \[as Barks suggested, incidentally, in *Walt Disney's Comics and Stories* #155 (August 1953)]. And I'm sure they will do a lot of good in the world, their Junior Woodchucks organization, they will save all the birds and all the whales.
items.value: CB: Oh well, because they are so much more practical than Donald. In the later stories, as I developed those Duck people and the whole community of Duckburg and all of its problems, I began giving those kids much more intelligence than anybody else in Duckburg. And so I guess that when Uncle Scrooge passes on, he will leave all of his money to his three nephews [as Barks suggested, incidentally, in Walt Disney's Comics and Stories #155 (August 1953)]. And I'm sure they will do a lot of good in the world, their Junior Woodchucks organization, they will save all the birds and all the whales.

items.md: **Sébastien Durand:** This is the first time that you have traveled outside the United States. You have seen many countries. How many countries have you visited to date?
items.value: Sébastien Durand: This is the first time that you have traveled outside the United States. You have seen many countries. How many countries have you visited to date?

items.md: **Carl Barks:** This is our number nine.
items.value: Carl Barks: This is our number nine.

items.md: **SD:** And you still have many left?
items.value: SD: And you still have many left?

items.md: **CB:** Yes, I have more to visit: Holland and England.
items.value: CB: Yes, I have more to visit: Holland and England.

items.md: **SD:** I understand that you had no passport to leave the United States.
items.value: SD: I understand that you had no passport to leave the United States.

items.md: **CB:** Well, I had never had any use for a passport before and at the time I was born people lived many miles apart. I guess that when I was born, there was word gotten to a doctor about five miles away to come out and deliver a baby at this homestead. Whether he recorded it or not. . . . There was no place to record it because the county governments were pretty
